name: springserve
documentation:
Springserve is a video adserver, and this library allows you
to interface with its api to do read/write and reporting requests

methods.name: springserve._common._BulkListResponse._to_list
documentation: The api needs a list, and you can't serialize sets, or Series

methods.name: springserve._common._BulkListResponse.bulk_create
documentation:
Appends this list to the existing list

    input_list: List of items to upload. Max 100k at a time
    file_path: file path to a csv of items.  This can be much larger (in the millions)

methods.name: springserve._common._BulkListResponse.bulk_replace
documentation:
Replaces the existing list with this list

    input_list: List of items to upload. Max 100k at a time
    file_path: file path to a csv of items.  This can be much larger (in the millions)

methods.name: springserve._common._BulkListResponse.bulk_delete
documentation: removes these items from the existing list

classes.name: springserve._common._AdvertiserDomainListResponse

methods.name: springserve._common._AdvertiserDomainListResponse.get_domains
documentation:
Get the list of domains that are in this domain list

    d = springserve.domain_list.get(id)
    domains = d.get_domains()

    for domain in domains:
        print domain.name

methods.name: springserve._common._AdvertiserDomainListResponse.add_domains
documentation:
Add a list of domains to this domain list

    d = springserve.domain_list.get(id)
    d.add_domains(['blah.com', 'blah2.com'])

domains: List of domains you would like to add

methods.name: springserve._common._AdvertiserDomainListResponse.remove_domains
documentation:
Add a list of domains to this domain list

    d = springserve.domain_list.get(id)
    d.remove_domains(['blah.com', 'blah2.com'])

domains: List of domains you would like to add
Override to give you access to the actual domains

classes.name: springserve._common._IpListResponse

methods.name: springserve._common._IpListResponse.get_ips
documentation:
Get the list of ips that are in this ip list

    d = springserve.ip_lists.get(id)
    ips = d.get_ips()

    for i in ips:
        print i.ip

methods.name: springserve._common._IpListResponse.add_ips
documentation:
Add a list of ips to this ip list

    d = springserve.ip_lists.get(id)
    d.add_ips(['123', '124'])

ips: List of ips you would like to add

methods.name: springserve._common._IpListResponse.remove_ips
documentation:
Remove a list of ips from this ip list

    d = springserve.ip_lists.get(id)
    d.remove_ips(['123', '124'])

ips: List of ips you would like to remove
Override to give you access to the actual ips

methods.name: springserve._common._BillItemAPI.endpoint
documentation:
The api endpoint that is used for this service.  For example:: 
    
    In [1]: import springserve

    In [2]: springserve.supply_tags.endpoint
    Out[2]: '/supply_tags'

functions.name: springserve.setup_config
documentation:
This is used the first time you run it to set up your configuration if
you want to do that over the command prompt

functions.name: springserve.API
documentation:
Get the raw API object.  This is rarely used directly by a client of this
library, but it used as an internal function

functions.name: springserve.V1API
documentation:
Get the raw API object.  This is rarely used directly by a client of this
library, but it used as an internal function

variables.name: springserve.__version__
documentation: TODO: This is duplicated in the build.  Need to figure how to set this once

classes.name: springserve.SpringServeAPITokenOverride
documentation: Used to inject a token instead of having the SDK do auth

classes.name: springserve._TabComplete
documentation:
this class exists to make any other class
have a tab completion function that is already
hooked into ipython

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIResponse.ok
documentation:
Tells you if the api response was "ok"

meaning that it responded with a 200.  If there was an error, this will
return false

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIResponse.raw
documentation:
Gives you the raw json response from the api.  Usually you do not need
to call this

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIResponse.__getattr__
documentation:
This is where the magic happens that allows you to treat this as an
object that has all of the fields that the api returns.  I seperate all
of the returned data in self._data

methods.name: springserve._VDAPISingleResponse.set_dirty
documentation:
you need this for nested fields that you have changed
but didn't actually set

methods.name: springserve._VDAPISingleResponse.save
documentation:
Save this object back to the api after making changes.  As an example:

```
tag = springserve.supply_tags.get(1)
tag.name = "This is my new name"
# this will print if the save went through correctly
print tag.save().ok
```

Returns:

> An API response object

methods.name: springserve._VDAPISingleResponse.__setattr__
documentation:
If it's a property that was already defined when the class
was initialized that let it exist.  If it's new than let's slap it into
_data.  This allows us to set new attributes and save it back to the api

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIMultiResponse.__iter__
documentation: this will automatically take care of pagination for us.

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIService.endpoint
documentation:
The api endpoint that is used for this service.  For example:

```
In [1]: import springserve

In [2]: springserve.supply_tags.endpoint
Out[2]: '/supply_tags'
```

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIService.get_raw
documentation:
Get the raw http response for this object.  This is rarely used by a
client unless they want to inspect the raw http fields

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIService.get
documentation:
Make a get request to this api service.  Allows you to pass in arbitrary
query paramaters.

Examples:

```
# get all supply_tags
tags = springserve.supply_tags.get()

for tag in tags:
    print tag.id, tag.name

# get one supply tag
tag = springserve.supply_tag.get(1)
print tag.id, tag.name

# get by many ids
tags = springserve.supply_tags.get(ids=[1,2,3])

# get users that are account_contacts (ie, using query string # params)
users = springserve.users.get(account_contact=True)
```

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIService.bulk_delete
documentation: Delete an object.

methods.name: springserve._VDAPIService.new
documentation:
Create a new object.  You need to pass in the required fields as a
dictionary.  For instance:

```
resp = springserve.domain_lists.new({'name':'My Domain List'})
print resp.ok
```

methods.name: springserve._reporting._ReportingAPI.run
documentation:
parameter     options (if applicable)  notes
===================================================
start_date:  "2015-12-01 00:00:00" or "2015-12-01"
end_date:    "2015-12-02 00:00:00" or "2015-12-01"
interval:    "hour", "day", "cumulative"
timezone:    "UTC", "America/New_York"   defaults to America/New_York
date_range:  Today, Yesterday, Last 7 Days   date_range takes precedence over start_date/end_date
dimensions:  supply_tag_id, demand_tag_id, detected_domain, declared_domain, demand_type, supply_type, supply_partner_id, demand_partner_id, supply_group  domain is only available when using date_range of Today, Yesterday, or Last 7 Days

the following parameters act as filters; pass an array of values (usually IDs)
=================================================================================

supply_tag_ids:  [22423,22375, 25463]
demand_tag_ids:  [22423,22375, 25463]
detected_domains:         ["nytimes.com", "weather.com"]
declared_domains:         ["nytimes.com", "weather.com"]
supply_types     ["Syndicated","Third-Party"]
supply_partner_ids:  [30,42,41]
supply_group_ids:    [13,15,81]
demand_partner_ids:  [3,10,81]
demand_types:    ["Vast Only","FLASH"]

modules.name: springserve._decorators
documentation:
AWS ELB 5XX error looks like: 503 Service Temporarily Unavailable

503 Service Temporarily Unavailable

functions.name: springserve._decorators.raw_response_retry
documentation:
Decorator for SpringServe API to handle retries (with exponential backoff) in the case
of a rate-limit or 5XX error.

Sleep duration and backoff factor control wait time between successive failures, e.g.
sleep_duration 3 and backoff_factor 2 means sleep 3s, 6s, 12s, 24s

:param int limit: Max number of retry attempts
:param int sleep_duration: Initial sleep time
:param float/int backoff_factor: Factor to increase sleep between successive retries.
